name: Cobham Services (M25)
description: Extra MSA, M25 J9-10. Good for the first stretch out of Lewes — Leon, M&S Food, big play area.

name: Watford Gap Services (M1)
description: Classic M1 stop, J16-17. Roadchef — decent toilets, Costa, Burger King.

name: Leicester Forest East Services (M1)
description: Welcome Break, M1 J21. Soft play if kids need to burn energy.

name: Donington Park Services (M1)
description: M1 J23A-24. Moto, with good EV chargers if Mum diverts via M1.

name: Trentham Gardens & Shopping Village
description: Just off the A50 near Stoke. Italian gardens, big lake walk, monkey forest, café and indie shops. Genuinely nice legstretch — kid-favourite.
from: ~4 hrs from Lewes · last big stop before the barnhouse

name: Washington Services (A1)
description: Moto, A1 just south of Newcastle. Quick first stop.

name: Wetherby Services (A1)
description: Moto, well rated. Costa, Burger King, big outdoor area for the chihuahua.

name: Woolley Edge Services (M1)
description: Moto, M1 J38-39. Has a small kids play area.

name: Chatsworth (diversion)
description: 20-min diversion off the A619 if you have time — stretch legs on the estate paths or grab a coffee.

name: Pavilion Gardens Car Park (Buxton)
description: Council-run rapid chargers (BP Pulse / Source London — verify on Zap-Map). Good for a top-up while grabbing coffee.

name: Spring Gardens Car Park (Buxton)
description: GeniePoint rapid chargers right by Tesco — combine with the Leek shop alternative.

name: Smithfield Centre Car Park (Leek)
description: Town-centre fast chargers — pair with the Morrisons Leek shop stop.
from: 8 mi from the barnhouse · best fit with arrival shop

name: Trentham Gardens BP Pulse
description: Decent fast chargers + gardens for a break. ~25 min south of the barnhouse on the A53.

name: Tesla Supercharger, Stafford
description: If Mum drives a Tesla — V3 chargers, easy detour off the M6.
